notes: Pickleheads lists exactly 1 Campbell, CA location -- The HUB Silicon Valley, 20 indoor courts -- confirmed via site:pickleheads.com/courts/us/california/campbell (only two URLs returned: the city index page and this one venue page). Campbell has no existing page/baseline in this repo, so every venue here is matchStatus:new. Cross-checking the official city site (campbellca.gov) plus 10+ third-party aggregators (DailyPB, Places2Play, PlayPickleball, PicklePlay, Bounce, GlobalPickleballNetwork, PickleballRush, TennisPickleballCalifornia, Chamber of Commerce, Yelp, HAQ Pickle) surfaced exactly one additional real public venue not on Pickleheads: Campbell Community Center's indoor Adult Open Gym pickleball program (portable nets on a gym hardwood floor, not dedicated pickleball courts). Both confirmed venues are indoor -- this research did not find any outdoor dedicated or tennis-striped pickleball courts anywhere in Campbell (the Community Center's own separate outdoor tennis courts, per campbellca.gov/1347/Tennis-Courts, are not used for pickleball). Deliberately excluded from venues: (1) John D. Morgan Park -- official city sources describe occasional pickleball EVENTS there (e.g. a July 20 tournament, a 'learn to play' celebration reportedly on the Pony Baseball Diamond) but no permanent or striped pickleball courts of its own; not a venue you can just show up and play at outside scheduled special events. (2) Paul's Pickleball Instruction (500 E Hamilton Ave, Campbell mailing address) -- a private coaching business (registered/compliant with the city of San Jose per its own listing) with no courts of its own; a service, not a venue. (3) 'Campbell Community Center Stadium' per aggregator HAQ Pickle -- that listing itself says court information 'is currently being verified'; treated as a low-quality duplicate/auto-scrape of the Community Center entry, not a real third location. (4) Swimply's Campbell-area search results -- private backyard court rentals, and the actual listings returned were not even in Campbell (Scotts Valley, Granite Creek Estates); out of scope as non-public listings outside the city. Also worth flagging: Pickleheads has a same-named 'Campbell Community Center' page, but it resolves to Campbell River, British Columbia, Canada -- a namesake collision (the same pattern as the Oakland NJ/CA collision already documented in this repo's pickleheads-court-research-prompts.md), not the California venue. The HUB Silicon Valley has a Planning-Commission-directed study (reported by San Jose Spotlight) of a 5-year conditional use permit for a 9-court expansion into adjacent former-warehouse space; not yet built as of this research date, so not counted in its court total -- worth a future re-check.

venues.name: The HUB Silicon Valley
venues.surface: Indoor hard court, 57,000 sq ft indoor facility (former Fry's Electronics store); 20 individually fenced, dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. One aggregator (TennisPickleballCalifornia) names the surfacing 'Acrytech.'
venues.price: Membership required; guest day pass $40 (all-day access, in-and-out privileges)
venues.skill: All levels, beginner to pro; DUPR-rated games, leagues, tournaments, clinics, social ladders, monthly themed events
venues.notes: Very well corroborated -- 10+ independent sources including the venue's own official site, Pickleheads, Yelp, DailyPB, Bounce, TennisPickleballCalifornia, GlobalPickleballNetwork, and Chamber of Commerce all agree on 20 indoor courts and membership-required access. One outlier, PicklePlay, describes this exact address as '20 outdoor courts ... no lighting,' which contradicts every other source (including the venue's own description of a 57,000 sq ft indoor building and Yelp reviews praising 'evenly lit courts'); treated as a PicklePlay data error, not a genuine open conflict, so confirmed stays true. Address suite formatting varies slightly by source ('#150' per the venue's own site, 'Ste 150' elsewhere, sometimes omitted) -- used the venue's own site's formatting. Coordinates are from Roadtrippers' listing for this exact venue/address, not independently re-geocoded by a second method, so treat as good-but-single-sourced. A Google Maps data= URL with a derivable hex place ID exists for the nearby Campbell Community Center but none was found for this venue at all, and no numeric cid could be independently confirmed for either -- googleMapsUrl left null per site convention rather than guessing. No Pickleheads-specific star rating found (Google shows roughly 4.0/105 reviews, Chamber of Commerce roughly 4.4/32 -- neither is a Pickleheads figure, so pickleheadsRating is left null). Booking/membership is managed via CourtReserve; bookingUrl is the venue's own CourtReserve login/portal link as published on its official site.

venues.name: Campbell Community Center
venues.surface: Indoor hardwood gymnasium floor with portable pickleball lines/nets -- not dedicated/permanent pickleball courts. Court count varies seasonally per aggregators: 4 courts in the Main Gym during the school year, 3 courts in the Auxiliary Gym during summer (both gyms confirmed indoor hardwood via the city's own facility pages, which describe the gyms generically and do not themselves mention pickleball).
venues.netType: Portable nets on hardwood gym floor
venues.price: $8.00 per session (per official city Sports page, fee effective June 1, 2026). One aggregator, Places2Play, instead states $4/session -- unresolved discrepancy; the official city page is treated as authoritative here.
venues.hours: Adult Open Gym "PM Pickleball": Sunday 6:30pm-9:45pm, ages 18+ (per official city Sports page, current cycle begins January 4). Places2Play instead lists Tuesday 8:30am-10:45am -- not corroborated by the official city page; may be stale or a separate/discontinued session, flagged rather than resolved. General gym rental hours (Main Gym/Auxiliary Gym, not pickleball-specific): Mon-Thu 8am-10pm, Fri 8am-10pm, Sat-Sun 8am-10pm.
venues.skill: All levels welcome; Places2Play lists beginner, 2.5, 3.0, and 3.5 groupings. Official program is Adult 18+; a differing '50+' age claim on PickleballRush is contradicted by the city's own 18+ program categorization and was not used.
venues.notes: Not listed on Pickleheads under Campbell, CA -- Pickleheads' city index for Campbell returns only The HUB Silicon Valley (1 location total). A Pickleheads page named 'Campbell Community Center' does exist but resolves to Campbell River, British Columbia, Canada -- a namesake collision, not this venue -- so pickleheadsUrl is left null rather than linking the wrong page. This is a gym-based Adult Open Gym program, not a dedicated pickleball facility: portable nets/lines set up on the Community Center's Main Gym or Auxiliary Gym hardwood floor. Neither gym's own city facility page mentions pickleball by name (they list basketball standards and volleyball court markings instead), but 5+ independent pickleball-specific aggregators consistently and specifically describe this exact address and program, and the city's own Sports page (409/Sports) directly confirms a 'PM Pickleball' Adult Open Gym session -- treated as confirmed on the strength of the primary source plus that corroboration. The Community Center's separate OUTDOOR tennis courts (4 courts near the Latimer Ave entrance, per campbellca.gov/1347/Tennis-Courts) are not used for pickleball and are a different facility from this entry. Registration is mandatory in advance online or in person (cash not accepted on-site, no walk-up drop-in), so 'reservable' is set to Reservable even though it is technically session sign-up rather than reserving a specific court. lat/lon are read directly from the numeric coordinate pair (!3d/!4d) embedded in a live Google Maps place URL for 'Campbell Community Center,' not independently re-geocoded by a second method. That same URL contains a hex place ID a CID could be mechanically derived from, but the derived value could not be independently verified to resolve to the correct page, so googleMapsUrl is left null per site convention rather than risk a wrong link. Excluded a duplicate-looking 'Campbell Community Center Stadium' listing from the aggregator HAQ Pickle, which itself states its court information 'is currently being verified' -- treated as a low-quality auto-scrape artifact of this same venue, not a genuine third location.
